title: chlorophora tinctoria var. chlorocarpa
intro: Chlorophora tinctoria var. chlorocarpa, also known as the heartleaf or black heart, is a variety of the Catechu tree, native to tropical forests in Asia and Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Chlorophora tinctoria var. chlorocarpa have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the bark and leaves of the tree.
